note: Senate returns from recess; merged Banking/Ag text expected this week; ethics deadlock on Trump crypto disclosure; only Gallego/Alsobrooks firm of 7 needed Dem votes; NDAA competing for floor time

Merged Banking/Agriculture draft released this week (+70 pages), but ethics provision omitted entirely; floor vote firming toward specific week-of-July-20 target; Wyden letter narrows Dem objection list slightly but no new firm votes beyond Gallego/Alsobrooks; new risk: shrinking GOP conference (Graham's death, McConnell absence) raises effective Dem-vote bar

Polymarket odds plummet to ~24-39% range (from peak >80% in Feb) despite Trump tying bill to China-competition warning; no new floor-time scheduling announced despite bill being one day closer to Aug 7 recess cutoff; Graham's death removes a GOP voice from the push; ethics, dem-vote count, and merged-text status unchanged from yesterday; market pricing now more bearish than the procedural sub-scores alone would suggest

Trump met with senators at White House Thursday afternoon to try to resolve the conflict-of-interest/ethics provision; no readout on outcome yet, unclear if Dems were part of the meeting; Thune reiterates hope for floor vote before Aug 7 recess but no firm date locked; merged text and Dem vote count (Gallego/Alsobrooks) unchanged; calendar runway ticks down another day; Polymarket odds still in the 24-39% range, down sharply from spring highs

Thursday's Trump-senator meeting produced no public deal; merged Banking/Ag draft confirmed to omit ethics language entirely, prompting Sens. Murphy, Van Hollen, and Merkley to formally oppose it, on top of the existing Gallego/Alsobrooks-only firm Dem count of 2/7; House Financial Services field hearing at Federal Hall today marks one year since House passage, but no Senate floor date announced; GENIUS Act stablecoin rulemaking deadline hits tomorrow (Jul 18), adding to a crowded agenda; Polymarket hit a record low of 32% today (from a Feb peak >80%), with other venues (Kalshi, Galaxy) showing similar or steeper declines
